evals.prompt: Compile my IEEE paper in main.tex, then explain why two citations are unresolved after BibTeX.
evals.expected_output: Select compile and bibliography modules, run the correct commands, and report build plus citation findings in LaTeX-oriented review format with exact failing steps or unresolved keys.

evals.prompt: Review the introduction section for grammar, sentence length, and logic, but do not touch equations or citation keys.
evals.expected_output: Route to grammar, sentences, and logic modules with source-preserving comments, keeping equations and citation keys untouched.

evals.prompt: Before I submit to ACM, check whether my figures are publication-ready and whether the title sounds too generic.
evals.expected_output: Route to figures and title modules and return concrete pre-submission findings about figure readiness, missing assets, resolution risks, or weak title wording.

evals.prompt: Translate this Chinese methods paragraph into academic English, but keep \\cite{zhang2024} and $L_{pred}$ unchanged and use deep-learning terminology.
evals.expected_output: Route to the translation module, preserve LaTeX fragments verbatim, surface terminology choices, and avoid silently rewriting citations or math.

evals.prompt: Check the introduction section for AI-sounding phrases and tell me what to revise, but do not rewrite labels, citations, or equations.
evals.expected_output: Route to the deai module and return source-preserving findings or risk notes without editing labels, citations, or math environments.

evals.prompt: Review the experiments section for weak baselines, unsupported claims, missing ablations, and absent significance reporting, but do not draft a replacement paragraph.
evals.expected_output: Route to the experiment module and return reviewer-style LaTeX comments about comparator specificity, metric evidence, ablation coverage, significance reporting, efficiency comparisons, and overclaiming.
